content:
General
* There are some existing Sudoku puzzles and one Latin Square puzzle ( https://www.codingame.com/training/medium/constrained-latin-squares ). The invalid grids probably make your contribution unique, but I'll need to think a bit more about whether it's truly distinct or too similar to existing puzzles.

Output
* Please format "Invalid" as a {{constant}} and remove the quotation marks.

* Please move the final sentence ("Whenever…") to the Constraints section.

Stub generator
* Please add a write statement, e.g. write answer.

Thanks.

Also, this is my first contribution to the community after many years using CodinGame. So please let me know if there's anything I should know about contributions to the community in general or improve in this puzzle specifically.

At first, I intended this puzzle to be a CoC reverse mode puzzle, but when I solved it myself and saw how involved the solution is, I realized it's probably a little too much for a CoC puzzle, let alone in reverse mode. 

So let me know: do you think this puzzle can be a CoC puzzle? Should I adapt it into a CoC puzzle?
Thank you for your feedback! Do you think this puzzle could be adapted to Clash of Code? (That's what I wanted originally) Or is it too much?

It might be a bit too much for a Clash of Code. A puzzle could work, but I'd like to hear what others think about the uniqueness/duplication issue first. It's also possible that modifying the puzzle further could resolve the problem. For now, let's wait a bit and see if we get more feedback.

After solving darkhorse64's puzzle, it seems to me it's more advanced than mine, for two main reasons:
1. The grid size goes up to 9x9
2. It requires counting all possible solutions (which there may be a lot of), rather than just finding one. 
Because of this, it requires a higher level of optimization than my puzzle. 

The same applies for the other Sudoku-related puzzles I reviewed. The most similar one I found is https://www.codingame.com/training/medium/sudoku-solver, which is rated medium difficulty, and involves solving a regular 9x9 Sudoku. I think my contribution might be the only one that fits the easy difficulty level. 
What do you think? Thanks again for the help!

There's also Mini Sudoku Solver ( https://www.codingame.com/training/hard/mini-sudoku-solver ; please ignore the misclassified difficulty).

I'm leaning towards your contribution being distinct enough, but I'd still like to hear what others think.

@danku9 - Unfortunately, Sudoku and Latin Squares are extremely well covered on the CodinGame platform. IMO, this puzzle is a slightly easier version of mini sudoku solver.

I like your idea of making it a clash of code. You’ll just need to figure out how to simplify the puzzle a bit more to make it fit the clash of code format. Maybe each grid only has a single empty cell? That might be too easy. How about two empty cells?
Thank you for the ideas! I'll consider it
